tasks.title: Setup Monorepo Structure and Base Configuration
tasks.description: Create the foundational monorepo structure with proper package organization and build tooling
tasks.details: Set up a monorepo using tools like Lerna or Nx with packages for core, parser, transformer, generator, validator, config, and plugins. Configure TypeScript with strict settings, ESLint, Prettier, and Jest. Create shared types and interfaces. Set up package.json scripts for building, testing, and publishing. Implement proper module resolution and dependency management between packages.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify all packages build successfully, TypeScript compilation works across packages, shared types are properly imported, and basic CLI structure is functional

subtasks.title: Configure workspace configuration
subtasks.description: Set up the monorepo workspace configuration using npm workspaces, defining the package structure and workspace-level dependencies
subtasks.details:
Create package.json with workspaces configuration, set up .npmrc for workspace management, configure hoisting rules, and establish workspace-level scripts for common operations
<info added on 2025-06-24T19:01:38.808Z>
Workspace configuration is now complete. Implemented npm workspaces in root package.json with proper workspace definitions. Configured .npmrc with appropriate hoisting rules for dependency management. Established base TypeScript configuration with composite project setup to support monorepo builds. Created complete package directory structure with individual packages: core (main library), parser (component parsing), transformer (code transformation), generator (output generation), validator (validation logic), config (configuration management), plugins (extensibility), and types (shared TypeScript definitions). Each package includes properly configured package.json files with correct dependencies and build scripts. Comprehensive TypeScript type definitions added to types package including UIKitAdapter interface for framework integration, WillowConfig schema for configuration validation, AST node types for parsing, Plugin interfaces for extensibility, Validation result types, Component/Style mapping definitions, and Transform operation interfaces. All packages are properly linked and ready for development.
</info added on 2025-06-24T19:01:38.808Z>

subtasks.title: Create package structure
subtasks.description: Establish the monorepo package structure with proper directory organization for different package types
subtasks.details: Create packages/ directory structure, set up individual package.json files for each package, define package naming conventions, and establish clear package boundaries and responsibilities

subtasks.title: Set up TypeScript configuration
subtasks.description: Configure TypeScript for the monorepo with proper module resolution and package references
subtasks.details: Create root tsconfig.json with composite projects, set up individual tsconfig.json files per package with proper references, configure path mappings for inter-package imports, and establish build order

subtasks.title: Configure linting and formatting
subtasks.description: Set up ESLint and Prettier with monorepo-aware configurations for consistent code style
subtasks.details: Install and configure ESLint with TypeScript support, set up Prettier with consistent formatting rules, create shared ESLint configs, configure lint-staged and husky for pre-commit hooks

subtasks.title: Set up testing framework
subtasks.description: Configure Jest or Vitest for monorepo testing with proper module resolution and coverage
subtasks.details: Install testing framework with TypeScript support, configure test runners for each package, set up shared test utilities, establish coverage thresholds, and create test scripts for individual and all packages

subtasks.title: Configure build tooling
subtasks.description: Set up build tools for compiling and bundling packages with proper dependency handling
subtasks.details: Configure build tools (tsup/esbuild/rollup) for package compilation, set up watch mode for development, create build scripts with proper dependency ordering, configure package exports and module formats

tasks.title: Define Core Interfaces and Type System
tasks.description: Create comprehensive TypeScript interfaces for all core abstractions including UI kit adapters, configuration schema, and plugin system
tasks.details: Define UIKitAdapter interface with methods for component transformation, style mapping, and validation. Create WillowConfig schema with support for UI kit selection, design system configuration, feature flags, and plugin definitions. Implement ComponentMap, StyleMap, TokenMap interfaces. Create AST node types and transformation interfaces. Use Zod or similar for runtime schema validation.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for interface compliance, schema validation tests with valid/invalid configurations, type checking tests to ensure interfaces work correctly across packages

subtasks.title: UIKitAdapter interface design
subtasks.description: Design the core UIKitAdapter interface that will serve as the contract for all UI kit integrations
subtasks.details: Create a comprehensive interface that defines methods for component transformation, style mapping, token resolution, and runtime validation. Include async support for dynamic imports and configuration options.

subtasks.title: WillowConfig schema definition
subtasks.description: Define the WillowConfig schema that will configure the design system behavior and UI kit integration
subtasks.details: Design a flexible configuration schema supporting multiple UI kits, custom tokens, component overrides, theme definitions, and build-time optimizations. Include TypeScript interfaces and validation schemas.

subtasks.title: Component mapping interfaces
subtasks.description: Create interfaces for mapping Willow components to target UI kit components
subtasks.details: Define types for component property mapping, event handler translation, slot/children handling, and component composition patterns. Support both one-to-one and one-to-many mappings.

subtasks.title: Style/token mapping interfaces
subtasks.description: Design interfaces for mapping design tokens and styles between Willow and target UI kits
subtasks.details: Create types for color token mapping, spacing scales, typography systems, breakpoint definitions, and custom CSS property handling. Include support for runtime theme switching.

subtasks.title: AST node type definitions
subtasks.description: Define TypeScript types for AST nodes used in code transformation
subtasks.details: Create comprehensive type definitions for JSX elements, import statements, component props, style objects, and template literals. Support TypeScript and JavaScript AST formats.

subtasks.title: Transformation interfaces
subtasks.description: Design interfaces for the transformation pipeline that converts Willow components to target UI kits
subtasks.details: Define types for transformation rules, visitor patterns, code generation options, and transformation context. Include hooks for custom transformations and middleware support.

subtasks.title: Runtime validation setup
subtasks.description: Set up runtime validation types and interfaces for development-time checks
subtasks.details: Create types for validation rules, error reporting, prop validation, style validation, and development warnings. Include integration with TypeScript's type system for compile-time checks.

tasks.title: Implement Configuration System
tasks.description: Build a robust configuration system that supports loading, merging, and validating configurations from multiple sources
tasks.details: Create configuration loader that supports JSON, YAML, and JavaScript config files. Implement configuration merging with precedence (global < project < CLI flags). Add environment variable support with WILLOW_ prefix. Create configuration validation using schemas from task 2. Support configuration inheritance and overrides. Implement caching for performance.
tasks.testStrategy: Test configuration loading from different file types, verify merging precedence works correctly, validate schema enforcement, test environment variable integration, and performance benchmarks

subtasks.title: Implement file loader for multiple formats
subtasks.description: Create a file loader that can read and parse configuration files in JSON, YAML, and JS/TS formats
subtasks.details: Build a flexible file loader module that detects file format based on extension and uses appropriate parsers. Support .json, .yaml, .yml, .js, and .ts extensions. Handle file reading errors gracefully and provide meaningful error messages.

subtasks.title: Build configuration merging logic with precedence rules
subtasks.description: Implement deep merging functionality that combines configurations from multiple sources according to defined precedence
subtasks.details: Create a deep merge utility that properly handles nested objects and arrays. Implement precedence order: CLI args > environment variables > local config files > global config files > defaults. Ensure later sources override earlier ones correctly.

subtasks.title: Add environment variable support and mapping
subtasks.description: Implement environment variable parsing and mapping to configuration properties
subtasks.details: Create a system to read environment variables with a specific prefix (e.g., WILLOW_) and map them to configuration paths. Support nested property mapping using delimiters (e.g., WILLOW_API_KEY maps to config.api.key). Handle type conversion for boolean and numeric values.

subtasks.title: Integrate validation system with configuration loading
subtasks.description: Connect the configuration loading system with the existing validation framework to ensure loaded configs are valid
subtasks.details: After merging configurations from all sources, pass the final configuration through the validation system. Provide clear error messages indicating which configuration source contains invalid values. Support partial validation for development environments.

subtasks.title: Implement configuration caching mechanism
subtasks.description: Add caching layer to avoid repeated file reads and parsing for unchanged configuration files
subtasks.details: Create a caching system that stores parsed configurations in memory. Implement cache invalidation based on file modification times. Add option to disable caching for development. Include cache warming functionality for production deployments.

tasks.title: Build AST Parser Module
tasks.description: Implement AST parsing capabilities using TypeScript compiler API for reliable code analysis
tasks.details: Use TypeScript compiler API (ts.createProgram, ts.createSourceFile) to parse TypeScript and JavaScript files. Implement component detection by analyzing JSX elements and React component patterns. Build import graph construction to track dependencies. Create scope analysis for proper symbol resolution. Handle edge cases like dynamic imports, re-exports, and conditional imports. Support both TS and JS with proper type stripping.
tasks.testStrategy: Test parsing of complex TypeScript/JavaScript files, verify component detection accuracy, validate import graph construction, test scope analysis with nested scopes, and benchmark parsing performance

subtasks.title: TypeScript compiler API setup
subtasks.description: Initialize and configure the TypeScript compiler API for the validation tool
subtasks.details: Set up the TypeScript compiler API, configure compiler options, create a program instance, and establish the basic infrastructure for parsing TypeScript/TSX files. Include proper error handling and configuration management.

subtasks.title: Component detection logic
subtasks.description: Implement logic to detect React components in TypeScript/JavaScript files
subtasks.details: Create detection patterns for function components, class components, arrow function components, and exported components. Handle various naming conventions and component declaration patterns including default exports and named exports.

subtasks.title: JSX element parsing
subtasks.description: Parse JSX elements to extract component usage and properties
subtasks.details: Implement JSX parsing to identify component usage, extract props, children, and nested components. Handle self-closing tags, fragments, and JSX expressions. Build a comprehensive AST traversal system for JSX elements.

subtasks.title: Import graph construction
subtasks.description: Build a dependency graph from import statements across the codebase
subtasks.details: Parse import statements to construct a directed graph of component dependencies. Handle various import types including default imports, named imports, namespace imports, and aliased imports. Track import paths and resolve relative/absolute paths.

subtasks.title: Scope analysis implementation
subtasks.description: Analyze variable scopes and component accessibility within modules
subtasks.details: Implement scope analysis to determine where components are accessible, handle module boundaries, track variable declarations and their usage contexts. Consider lexical scoping, module exports, and closure contexts.

subtasks.title: Edge case handling
subtasks.description: Handle complex scenarios like dynamic imports, re-exports, and conditional exports
subtasks.details: Implement handling for dynamic imports, barrel exports, re-exports, conditional exports, lazy loading, and code splitting scenarios. Handle TypeScript specific features like type exports and namespace merging.

subtasks.title: Type stripping for JS
subtasks.description: Process JavaScript files by stripping TypeScript type annotations
subtasks.details: Implement type stripping functionality to handle JavaScript files that may contain JSX but no TypeScript types. Ensure compatibility with both .js/.jsx and .ts/.tsx files. Handle flow type annotations if present.

subtasks.title: Parser optimization
subtasks.description: Optimize parsing performance for large codebases
subtasks.details: Implement caching mechanisms, incremental parsing, parallel processing where possible, and memory optimization. Add progress reporting for long-running operations and implement early termination for validation passes.

tasks.title: Create AST-Based Transformer Module
tasks.description: Replace regex-based transformations with reliable AST transformations for code modifications
tasks.details: Implement AST transformers using TypeScript compiler API transformer functions. Create import path resolution and rewriting logic. Build component prop mapping transformations. Implement style transformation capabilities. Handle complex scenarios like default imports, namespace imports, and aliased imports. Create rollback mechanisms for failed transformations. Support both in-place and copy transformations. Style transformation engine is significantly advanced with 8 out of 10 subtasks complete, including CSS-in-JS parsers/transformers, Tailwind class transformer, CSS modules handler, theme token migration, property renaming engine, styled-components transformer, emotion transformer, and style validation/optimization system (90% complete).
tasks.testStrategy: Test import transformations with various import patterns, verify component prop mapping accuracy, validate style transformations, test rollback mechanisms, and compare performance against regex-based approach. Focus on completing style transformation test fixes and comprehensive end-to-end testing.

subtasks.title: Design transformer API interface
subtasks.description: Define the core API structure for transformation operations including method signatures, configuration options, and error handling patterns
subtasks.details: Create TypeScript interfaces for transformer classes, define configuration schema, establish error handling patterns, and design plugin architecture for extensibility

subtasks.title: Implement import transformation logic
subtasks.description: Build the core logic to parse and transform import statements using AST manipulation
subtasks.details: Use TypeScript compiler API or Babel to parse import statements, identify source modules, transform import paths, and handle various import syntaxes (default, named, namespace)

subtasks.title: Implement style transformation engine
subtasks.description: Build system to transform CSS-in-JS styles, class names, and style properties
subtasks.details:
Style transformation engine is significantly advanced with 8 out of 10 subtasks complete. All major components implemented: CSS-in-JS parsers/transformers, Tailwind class transformer, CSS modules handler, theme token migration, property renaming engine, styled-components transformer, emotion transformer, and style validation/optimization system (90% complete with robust architecture). Currently focused on completing test fixes for subtask 5.4.9 and then moving to 5.4.10 for comprehensive end-to-end style transformation tests.
<info added on 2025-06-25T08:18:25.853Z>
Implementation Status: Style validation and optimization system is 90% complete with robust architecture in place. Built BaseStyleValidator with pluggable validation rules covering CSS properties, values, colors, units, best practices, and accessibility checks. BaseStyleOptimizer implemented with comprehensive strategies: duplicate removal, rule merging, property sorting, shorthand conversion, color optimization, unit optimization, and aggressive optimizations. Factory functions created for custom validators and optimizers. System supports validation severity levels (ERROR, WARNING, INFO) and optimization levels (NONE, BASIC, STANDARD, AGGRESSIVE). Test suite contains 29 tests with 26 passing - only 3 minor failures remain for magic number detection, z-index warnings, and color optimization edge cases. Core architecture functional and ready for integration.
</info added on 2025-06-25T08:18:25.853Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Focus on completing the remaining 3 test failures in the validation/optimization system, then proceed with comprehensive end-to-end style transformation testing

subtasks.title: Build namespace and alias handling
subtasks.description: Implement logic to handle import namespaces, aliases, and dynamic import patterns
subtasks.details: Support star imports, aliased imports, re-exports, and barrel exports. Maintain import reference tracking across transformed files

subtasks.title: Develop rollback mechanism
subtasks.description: Create safe rollback system to undo transformations if issues occur
subtasks.details: Implement backup creation, change tracking, validation system, and restoration process. Include dry-run mode for preview before applying changes

subtasks.title: Build in-place vs copy transformation modes
subtasks.description: Implement options for transforming files in-place or creating copies
subtasks.details: Add configuration for transformation mode, implement file copying logic, handle file permissions, and provide progress tracking for both modes

subtasks.title: Create transformer composition system
subtasks.description: Build system to chain and compose multiple transformers for complex migration scenarios
subtasks.details: Design transformer pipeline architecture, implement transformer ordering, handle inter-transformer dependencies, and provide composition configuration options

subtasks.title: Complete style validation/optimization test fixes
subtasks.description: Fix the remaining 3 failing tests in the style validation and optimization system
subtasks.details: Address the minor test failures for magic number detection, z-index warnings, and color optimization edge cases. The system is 90% complete with 26 out of 29 tests passing. These fixes are needed to finalize the validation/optimization system before moving to end-to-end testing.

subtasks.title: Create comprehensive end-to-end style transformation tests

subtasks.details: Develop comprehensive end-to-end tests that validate the complete style transformation pipeline from input to output. Include tests for CSS-in-JS transformations, Tailwind class migrations, CSS modules handling, theme token migrations, and property renaming. Test integration between all components and validate performance under real-world scenarios.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create test scenarios covering all major transformation types, integration tests between components, performance benchmarks, and edge case handling across the entire style transformation pipeline

subtasks.title: Create Component Property Mapping System

subtasks.details: Build mapping configuration system, implement prop name transformations, handle prop value conversions, and manage deprecated prop warnings. subtasks.title: Design component mapping configuration schema

subtasks.title: Implement prop name transformation engine
subtasks.description: Build core transformation logic
subtasks.details: Develop the engine that transforms property names based on mapping rules. Support camelCase to kebab-case conversions, prefix/suffix additions, and custom transformation functions. Handle nested property paths and spread operators. Unit tests required: Name transformation accuracy tests, edge case handling (special characters, numbers), performance tests for bulk transformations, nested property path resolution tests. subtasks.title: Create prop value converter system

Support async converters for complex transformations. Unit tests required: Type conversion accuracy tests, unit conversion tests with edge cases, color format conversion tests (hex, rgb, hsl), custom converter integration tests. subtasks.title: Build prop deprecation handler

Include deprecation timeline tracking and reporting. Unit tests required: Deprecation detection tests, warning message generation tests, auto-migration accuracy tests, deprecation report generation tests. subtasks.title: Implement conditional prop mapping
subtasks.description: Handle conditional transformations

Support complex conditions with AND/OR logic and nested conditions. Unit tests required: Conditional logic evaluation tests, context-based mapping tests, complex condition resolution tests, performance tests with many conditions. subtasks.title: Create prop validation system
subtasks.description: Validate transformed props
subtasks.details: Build validation system that checks transformed properties against target component prop types, required props, and value constraints. Include runtime and compile-time validation options. Unit tests required: Prop type validation tests, required prop detection tests, value constraint validation tests, custom validator integration tests. subtasks.title: Build prop migration analyzer

Git commit required: 'feat(transformer): complete prop migration analyzer - 5.3.7'

subtasks.title: Implement bulk prop transformation
subtasks.description: Handle multiple components

Include memory optimization for large codebases. subtasks.title: Create prop mapping test suite

Include test data generators and fixtures. Unit tests required: Edge case coverage tests, performance benchmark suite, integration test suite with multiple UI kits, regression test suite. subtasks.title: Build prop mapping documentation generator

tasks.title: Implement UI Kit Adapter System
tasks.description: Create the UI kit abstraction layer with adapters for popular UI libraries
tasks.details: Implement the UIKitAdapter interface defined in task 2. Create comprehensive adapters for multiple UI libraries including Radix UI, Material-UI, AriaKit, BaseUI, Ant Design, and Bootstrap with component mapping, style translation, and token conversion. Implement component mapping logic that handles prop differences, naming conventions, and API variations. Create style system translations between different CSS-in-JS and utility frameworks. Continue development on ShadcnAdapter and complete remaining adapter implementations.
tasks.testStrategy: Test each adapter with sample components, verify component mapping accuracy, validate style translations, test token conversions, and create integration tests with real UI kit components

subtasks.title: Build Willow reference adapter
subtasks.description: Implement the reference adapter for Willow Design System as the baseline implementation
subtasks.details:
Create mappings for all Willow components, establish patterns for prop conversion, and document the reference implementation approach that other adapters will follow.
<info added on 2025-06-25T07:49:26.307Z>
The Willow adapter should be removed as Willow serves as the canonical format that other UI libraries adapt to, not the reverse. Instead, implement example adapters that demonstrate bidirectional conversion between popular UI libraries (such as Material-UI to Ant Design, or Chakra UI to Mantine) to showcase the adapter system's capabilities for cross-library component migration and interoperability.
</info added on 2025-06-25T07:49:26.307Z>
<info added on 2025-06-25T08:16:03.880Z>
Status: Needs reconsideration. This subtask should pivot from creating a Willow-specific adapter to implementing example adapters that demonstrate bidirectional conversion between popular UI libraries (e.g., Material-UI ↔ Ant Design, Chakra UI ↔ Mantine). These examples will better showcase the adapter system's capabilities for cross-library component migration and interoperability, since Willow serves as the canonical format that other libraries adapt to.
</info added on 2025-06-25T08:16:03.880Z>

subtasks.title: Complete component mapping engine
subtasks.description: Finish the dynamic mapping system that translates between different component APIs
subtasks.details:
Complete implementation of prop transformation rules, event handler mapping, children/slot conversion, ref forwarding strategies, and a plugin system for custom mappings. Build on the existing base architecture to create the core mapping functionality that makes components interoperable between UI kits.
<info added on 2025-06-25T07:49:58.415Z>
Status Update: Base adapter architecture implemented with mapComponent method and ComponentMapping interface established. Foundation is complete but core mapping functionality still requires implementation.

Remaining Implementation Tasks:
1. Prop transformation rules engine - Need to build dynamic property mapping system that can handle type conversions, name transformations, and conditional prop passing
2. Event handler mapping system - Implement event name translation and handler wrapping to normalize different UI kit event patterns
3. Children/slot conversion logic - Create system to handle different child content patterns (React children vs Vue slots vs Angular content projection)
4. Ref forwarding strategies - Develop consistent ref handling across different component architectures and frameworks

The architectural foundation provides the structure, but the actual adaptation logic that makes components interoperable between UI kits is the next critical implementation phase.
</info added on 2025-06-25T07:49:58.415Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Test prop transformation rules, event handler mappings, and children conversion with multiple UI kit combinations

subtasks.title: Complete style system translation layer
subtasks.description: Finish the style transformation system to convert between different styling approaches
subtasks.details:
Complete the integration between the transformer package's style transformation capabilities and the adapter's runtime style translation system. Build converters for CSS-in-JS to utility classes, theme token mapping, responsive breakpoint translation, animation/transition handling, and dark mode compatibility.
<info added on 2025-06-25T07:50:29.880Z>
The base adapter architecture is now in place with the translateStyles method and StyleConfig interface implemented. The transformer package provides extensive style transformation capabilities including parsers and transformers for Tailwind, CSS-in-JS, and styled-components. The key remaining work is to integrate the transformer's style transformation capabilities with the adapter's runtime style translation system. The token conversion system has been successfully implemented with type-safe helpers, providing a foundation for mapping design tokens between different UI kits. Next steps involve creating the integration layer between the static transformation capabilities and runtime style translation.
</info added on 2025-06-25T07:50:29.880Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Test style transformations between different CSS approaches, validate theme token mappings, and verify dark mode compatibility

subtasks.title: Implement base adapter architecture
subtasks.description: Create the foundational adapter interface and abstract base class that all UI library adapters will extend
subtasks.details:
Define core adapter methods for component creation, prop mapping, event handling, and style transformation. Include TypeScript interfaces for type safety and plugin hooks for extensibility.
<info added on 2025-06-25T07:36:53.693Z>
Successfully implemented comprehensive adapter architecture with the following components:

- AdapterTypes.ts: 30+ TypeScript interfaces defining core adapter contracts
- AdapterRegistry: Singleton pattern for managing adapter instances
- AdapterFactory: Component creation with built-in caching mechanism
- AdapterValidator: JSON Schema-based validation system
- AdapterContext: React Context API integration for state management
- AdapterError: Custom error handling system with specific error types
- AdapterPluginManager: Extensible plugin system for additional functionality
- AccessibilityPlugin: Built-in accessibility features and compliance checks
- Comprehensive test suite: 157 passing tests covering all components
- Test fixtures and token helpers for streamlined testing
- Full integration test suite validating end-to-end adapter functionality

All core adapter methods implemented including component creation, prop mapping, event handling, and style transformation with full TypeScript type safety and plugin extensibility hooks.
</info added on 2025-06-25T07:36:53.693Z>

subtasks.title: Develop Radix UI adapter

subtasks.details:
Map Willow components to Radix primitives, handle Radix's composition model, implement accessibility features, and manage controlled/uncontrolled component states.
<info added on 2025-06-25T07:55:20.145Z>
Successfully implemented RadixAdapter with comprehensive component support including Dialog, Select, Accordion, Slider, Switch, Tooltip, Popover, and Form. The adapter handles Radix's unique patterns: compound components (Dialog.Root, Dialog.Content, etc.), asChild prop for composition, portal rendering, and dual controlled/uncontrolled state management. </info added on 2025-06-25T07:55:20.145Z>

subtasks.title: Implement output format support
subtasks.description: Add support for multiple output formats and code styles
subtasks.details: Enable generation of code in different formats, languages, and coding standards. Include configuration options for output customization.

subtasks.title: Integrate Prettier for code formatting
subtasks.description: Add Prettier integration for automatic code formatting and style consistency
subtasks.details: Integrate Prettier to ensure all generated code follows consistent formatting rules. Configure Prettier options and handle formatting errors gracefully.

tasks.title: Create Validation Module
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive validation system for code quality, design system compliance, and dependency checks
tasks.details: Build validation rules engine that checks design system compliance, component usage patterns, and code quality. Implement dependency validation to ensure proper import structure. Create performance checks for bundle size impact. Build extensible rule system that supports custom validation rules. Integrate with UI kit adapters for kit-specific validation. Add validation reporting with actionable suggestions.
tasks.testStrategy: Test validation rules against known good/bad code examples, verify design system compliance checking, test dependency validation accuracy, benchmark performance checks, and validate custom rule integration

subtasks.title: Implement Validation Rules Engine
subtasks.description: Build the core rules engine that processes and executes validation rules
subtasks.details: Create a flexible engine that can load, parse, and execute different types of validation rules. Support rule chaining, conditional execution, and priority-based rule ordering. Include interfaces for rule definitions and execution contexts.

subtasks.title: Add Design System Compliance Checks
subtasks.description: Create validators that ensure components follow design system guidelines
subtasks.details: Implement checks for color tokens, spacing values, typography scales, component variants, and naming conventions. Validate against design tokens and component specifications defined in the design system.

subtasks.title: Implement Dependency Validation
subtasks.description: Build validators to check component dependencies and imports
subtasks.details: Validate import paths, check for circular dependencies, ensure proper dependency versioning, and verify that components only import allowed dependencies based on their layer in the architecture.

subtasks.title: Create Performance Checks
subtasks.description: Develop performance-related validation rules
subtasks.details: Implement checks for bundle size limits, component complexity metrics, render performance patterns, and memory usage patterns. Include static analysis for common performance anti-patterns.

subtasks.title: Build Custom Rule Support System
subtasks.description: Create API and infrastructure for users to define custom validation rules
subtasks.details: Design extensible API for custom rule creation, support rule configuration via JSON/YAML, implement rule plugin system, and provide helpers for common validation patterns. Include documentation generator for custom rules.

subtasks.title: Develop Validation Reporting System
subtasks.description: Build comprehensive reporting for validation results
subtasks.details: Create multiple output formats (JSON, HTML, Markdown), implement severity levels and error categorization, generate actionable fix suggestions, and provide progress tracking. Include CI/CD integration formats.

tasks.title: Implement Plugin Architecture
tasks.description: Create a flexible plugin system with lifecycle hooks and third-party plugin support
tasks.details: Define plugin API with lifecycle hooks (beforeTransform, afterTransform, beforeGenerate, afterGenerate). Implement plugin loader with discovery mechanisms (package.json, file system). Create plugin communication system for inter-plugin coordination. Build plugin lifecycle management with proper initialization and cleanup. Support both synchronous and asynchronous plugins. Implement plugin configuration and dependency management.
tasks.testStrategy: Test plugin loading and initialization, verify lifecycle hooks execute correctly, test plugin communication, validate configuration handling, and create example plugins for integration testing

subtasks.title: Define plugin API and interface structure
subtasks.description: Create TypeScript interfaces and types for the plugin system API, including base plugin interface, metadata structure, and hook definitions
subtasks.details: Design the core plugin interface with methods like initialize(), activate(), deactivate(), and cleanup(). Define metadata structure for plugin name, version, dependencies, and capabilities. Create type definitions for plugin hooks and extension points.

subtasks.title: Implement plugin lifecycle hooks
subtasks.description: Build the hook system that allows plugins to tap into various lifecycle events and extend functionality
subtasks.details: Implement beforeLoad, afterLoad, beforeActivate, afterActivate, beforeDeactivate, afterDeactivate hooks. Create a hook registry system with priority ordering. Add error handling for hook failures.

subtasks.title: Create plugin loader and discovery mechanism
subtasks.description: Implement the system for discovering, loading, and registering plugins from various sources
subtasks.details: Build plugin discovery from node_modules, local directories, and remote sources. Implement dynamic import for ES modules and CommonJS compatibility. Add plugin validation and verification before loading.

subtasks.title: Build inter-plugin communication system
subtasks.description: Develop a messaging and event system for plugins to communicate with each other and the core system
subtasks.details: Create an event bus for plugin-to-plugin communication. Implement a service registry for plugins to expose APIs to other plugins. Add request/response patterns with timeout handling.

subtasks.title: Implement plugin lifecycle management
subtasks.description: Create the core system for managing plugin states, transitions, and runtime control
subtasks.details: Build state machine for plugin lifecycle (installed, loaded, active, inactive, unloaded). Implement hot-reload capability for development. Add graceful shutdown and cleanup procedures.

subtasks.title: Add async plugin operation support
subtasks.description: Ensure all plugin operations properly handle asynchronous initialization, activation, and communication
subtasks.details: Convert all plugin operations to support Promises and async/await. Implement parallel plugin loading with dependency resolution. Add timeout handling for long-running plugin operations.

subtasks.title: Create plugin configuration and dependency resolution
subtasks.description: Build the system for managing plugin configuration, dependencies, and conflict resolution
subtasks.details: Implement plugin dependency graph resolution. Create configuration schema validation for plugins. Add version compatibility checking and conflict detection. Build plugin isolation for security.

tasks.title: Build Error Handling and Recovery System
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive error handling with rollback mechanisms and detailed error reporting
tasks.details: Create error boundaries for each module with proper error propagation. Implement rollback mechanisms that can restore original state on failures. Build detailed error reporting with context, suggestions, and recovery options. Add dry-run mode for all operations to preview changes. Implement operation logging for debugging and audit trails. Create error recovery strategies for common failure scenarios.
tasks.testStrategy: Test error boundary functionality, verify rollback mechanisms restore correct state, validate error message quality and actionability, test dry-run mode accuracy, and benchmark error handling performance

subtasks.title: Implement Error Boundary Architecture
subtasks.description: Create a comprehensive error boundary system to catch and handle errors at different levels of the application
subtasks.details: Design and implement error boundary components that can wrap critical sections of code. Include error state management, fallback UI components, and error context providers. Ensure proper error isolation to prevent cascading failures.

subtasks.title: Design Rollback Mechanism
subtasks.description: Create a transactional rollback system that can undo operations when errors occur
subtasks.details: Implement a state snapshot system before critical operations. Design rollback strategies for different operation types (file changes, database updates, etc.). Include version control integration for file-based rollbacks and transaction management for database operations.

subtasks.title: Build Error Reporting System
subtasks.description: Develop a comprehensive error reporting and tracking system with detailed error information
subtasks.details: Create error collection service with categorization (critical, warning, info). Include stack trace capture, user context, and operation history. Implement error aggregation and deduplication. Add integration points for external error tracking services.

subtasks.title: Implement Dry-Run Mode
subtasks.description: Create a simulation mode that validates operations without executing them
subtasks.details: Build operation simulation framework that validates all steps without side effects. Include comprehensive validation reporting showing what would happen. Create dry-run flags for CLI commands and API endpoints. Ensure accurate prediction of operation outcomes.

subtasks.title: Create Operation Logging System
subtasks.description: Implement detailed logging for all operations with structured log format
subtasks.details: Design structured logging with operation IDs, timestamps, and context. Include before/after states for critical operations. Implement log levels and filtering. Create log rotation and archival system. Ensure sensitive data is properly masked in logs.

subtasks.title: Develop Recovery Strategies
subtasks.description: Create automated and manual recovery procedures for different failure scenarios
subtasks.details: Design recovery strategies for common failure patterns. Implement automated recovery for transient failures with retry logic. Create manual recovery tools and documentation. Include data consistency checks and repair utilities. Build recovery testing framework.

tasks.title: Create CLI Interface and Commands
tasks.description: Build the command-line interface with intuitive commands and proper argument handling
tasks.details: Implement CLI using Commander.js or similar with commands for transform, validate, migrate, and config. Add support for various input formats (single files, directories, globs). Implement progress reporting and interactive prompts. Create help system with examples and usage patterns. Add global and local configuration support. Implement verbose logging and debug modes.
tasks.testStrategy: Test all CLI commands with various input combinations, verify argument parsing and validation, test progress reporting accuracy, validate help system completeness, and test configuration integration

subtasks.title: Implement core command structure and registry
subtasks.description: Create the foundational command system with a registry for all available commands, including command metadata, aliases, and execution framework
subtasks.details: Set up base command interface, command registry pattern, and command execution pipeline. Include support for command aliases, descriptions, and basic metadata. Implement command discovery and registration mechanisms.

subtasks.title: Build argument parsing and validation system
subtasks.description: Develop comprehensive argument parsing with type validation, required/optional flags, positional arguments, and error handling
subtasks.details: Implement argument parser that handles flags, options, positional arguments, and subcommands. Add type validation, custom validators, default values, and comprehensive error messages. Support both short and long flag formats.

subtasks.title: Create progress reporting and feedback mechanisms
subtasks.description: Implement progress bars, spinners, status messages, and real-time feedback for long-running operations
subtasks.details: Build progress reporting utilities including progress bars for determinate operations, spinners for indeterminate tasks, and status message updates. Ensure proper terminal handling and cleanup on interruption.

subtasks.title: Develop interactive prompt system
subtasks.description: Build interactive prompts for user input including text input, selections, confirmations, and multi-step wizards
subtasks.details: Create prompt utilities for various input types: text input with validation, single/multi-select lists, yes/no confirmations, and password input. Support input history, autocomplete, and multi-step wizard flows.

subtasks.title: Implement comprehensive help system and documentation
subtasks.description: Create auto-generated help documentation, usage examples, command discovery features, and integrated help command
subtasks.details: Build help system that auto-generates documentation from command metadata. Include usage examples, flag descriptions, subcommand listings, and contextual help. Add search functionality and 'did you mean' suggestions for typos.

tasks.title: Implement Comprehensive Testing Suite
tasks.description: Create extensive test coverage including unit tests, integration tests, and performance benchmarks
tasks.details: Write unit tests for all modules achieving >90% code coverage. Create integration tests that test end-to-end workflows with real codebases. Build performance benchmarks comparing new implementation to existing regex-based approach. Implement snapshot testing for code generation consistency. Create test fixtures for various UI kits and component patterns. Set up continuous integration with automated testing.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify >90% code coverage across all modules, validate integration tests cover main user workflows, confirm performance benchmarks show expected improvements, test snapshot consistency, and ensure CI pipeline catches regressions

subtasks.title: Unit test framework setup
subtasks.description: Set up and configure the unit testing framework (Jest/Vitest) with TypeScript support
subtasks.details: Install testing framework, configure test runners, set up test file patterns, configure TypeScript paths and module resolution for tests

subtasks.title: Integration test suite
subtasks.description: Create integration tests for CLI commands and component validation workflows
subtasks.details: Test full command execution flows, file system operations, validation logic integration, and error handling scenarios

subtasks.title: Performance benchmarks
subtasks.description: Implement performance benchmarking suite for validation and analysis operations
subtasks.details: Create benchmarks for file parsing speed, validation performance, memory usage tracking, and large codebase handling

subtasks.title: Snapshot testing setup
subtasks.description: Configure snapshot testing for CLI output and validation results
subtasks.details: Set up snapshot testing for command outputs, error messages, validation reports, and formatted output consistency

subtasks.title: Test fixture creation
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive test fixtures representing various component patterns and edge cases
subtasks.details: Build fixtures for valid/invalid components, complex import scenarios, different framework patterns, and edge case handling

subtasks.title: Code coverage configuration
subtasks.description: Set up code coverage reporting and establish coverage thresholds
subtasks.details: Configure coverage tools, set minimum thresholds, exclude non-testable files, integrate coverage reports with development workflow

subtasks.title: CI/CD pipeline setup
subtasks.description: Configure continuous integration pipeline for automated testing and validation
subtasks.details: Set up GitHub Actions workflows, configure test running on PRs, automate coverage reporting, integrate performance regression detection

tasks.title: Set up comprehensive pre-commit hooks with husky, lint-staged, vitest, ts-prune, and typedoc
tasks.description: Configure automated pre-commit hooks to enforce code quality, run tests, detect unused code, and generate documentation before each commit
tasks.details: Install and configure husky for Git hooks management. Set up lint-staged to run targeted checks only on staged files. Configure ESLint and Prettier integration for code formatting and linting. Set up vitest to run affected tests based on changed files. Integrate ts-prune to detect and report unused exports and dead code. Configure typedoc to generate API documentation from TypeScript source files. Create .husky/pre-commit hook that orchestrates all tools. Configure lint-staged in package.json with file pattern matching for different tools. Ensure hooks are fast enough to not impede developer workflow (target <10s for average commits). Add bypass mechanism for emergencies (--no-verify flag documentation). Create npm scripts for manual execution of each tool. Document the pre-commit workflow in contributing guidelines.
tasks.testStrategy: Test pre-commit hooks by making intentional code quality violations and verifying they're caught. Verify lint-staged only processes staged files, not entire codebase. Test vitest integration by breaking tests and confirming commit is blocked. Validate ts-prune catches unused exports by adding dead code. Confirm typedoc generates documentation correctly and catches documentation errors. Benchmark hook execution time on various commit sizes. Test bypass mechanism works when needed. Verify all tools work in CI environment as well as locally.

subtasks.title: Install and configure husky with initial Git hooks setup
subtasks.description: Install husky as a dev dependency and initialize Git hooks infrastructure
subtasks.details:
Install husky@latest using npm/yarn/pnpm. Run husky install to set up Git hooks. Configure package.json with prepare script to auto-install hooks. Create .husky directory structure. Verify hooks are properly installed by checking .git/hooks directory. Test basic hook functionality with a simple echo command.
<info added on 2025-06-26T01:29:52.842Z>
Nano-task breakdown:

1. Install husky package - Run `npm install --save-dev husky` or equivalent package manager command
2. Run husky install command - Execute `npx husky install` to initialize husky in the project
3. Add prepare script to package.json - Add `"prepare": "husky install"` to scripts section for automatic setup
4. Create initial .husky directory structure - Ensure .husky folder exists with proper permissions and basic structure
5. Verify Git hooks integration - Check that .git/hooks directory contains husky references and hooks are properly linked
6. Create test pre-commit hook with echo - Add simple `echo "Pre-commit hook working"` test hook to validate setup before adding real linting rules
</info added on 2025-06-26T01:29:52.842Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Create a test commit to verify husky is intercepting Git operations. Check that .husky directory exists and contains proper executable scripts.

subtasks.title: Set up lint-staged with ESLint and Prettier configuration
subtasks.description: Install and configure lint-staged to run code formatting and linting on staged files only
subtasks.details:
Install lint-staged, eslint, prettier, and related configs as dev dependencies. Create .lintstagedrc.json with file pattern matching rules. Configure ESLint with appropriate rules for TypeScript. Set up Prettier with project-specific formatting rules. Define lint-staged tasks for .ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx files to run eslint --fix and prettier --write. Ensure proper integration between ESLint and Prettier to avoid conflicts.
<info added on 2025-06-26T01:30:20.502Z>
Nano-task breakdown for implementation:

1. Install lint-staged, eslint, prettier packages - Run npm install --save-dev lint-staged eslint prettier @typescript-eslint/parser @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin eslint-config-prettier eslint-plugin-prettier

2. Create .eslintrc.json with TypeScript rules - Set up extends array with @typescript-eslint/recommended and prettier, configure parser and parserOptions for TypeScript, add appropriate rules for code quality

3. Create .prettierrc with formatting rules - Define consistent formatting: semi: true, singleQuote: true, tabWidth: 2, trailingComma: 'es5', printWidth: 80, endOfLine: 'lf'

4. Create .lintstagedrc.json configuration - Structure with file pattern keys and command arrays as values

5. Configure file pattern matching for different file types - Map "*.{ts,tsx}": ["eslint --fix", "prettier --write"], "*.{js,jsx}": ["eslint --fix", "prettier --write"], "*.{json,md}": ["prettier --write"]

6. Test lint-staged with sample staged files - Create test files, stage them, run npx lint-staged to verify commands execute correctly on staged files only

7. Add lint-staged to husky pre-commit hook - Update .husky/pre-commit to include npx lint-staged command after existing checks
</info added on 2025-06-26T01:30:20.502Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Stage various file types and run lint-staged manually to verify correct tool execution. Test with intentionally malformed code to ensure linting catches errors.

subtasks.title: Configure vitest for pre-commit test execution
subtasks.description: Set up vitest to run only tests affected by staged changes
subtasks.details:
Configure vitest with --related flag to run only tests related to changed files. Create custom script that identifies test files based on staged changes. Set up vitest config to run in watch=false mode for pre-commit. Configure test coverage thresholds if applicable. Add lint-staged configuration to run vitest for test files and related source files. Optimize test execution time by using vitest's built-in parallelization.
<info added on 2025-06-26T01:30:52.606Z>
**Nano-tasks breakdown:**

1. Create vitest pre-commit script for affected tests
   - Write shell script or Node.js script to execute vitest commands
   - Handle exit codes properly for pre-commit hook integration
   - Add error handling and logging

2. Configure vitest with --related flag setup
   - Update vitest.config.ts to support related test detection
   - Configure test file patterns and source file mappings
   - Set up dependency graph analysis for accurate test selection

3. Create custom test detection logic for staged files
   - Implement logic to parse git staged files
   - Map source files to corresponding test files
   - Handle edge cases like renamed/moved files

4. Add vitest configuration for non-watch mode
   - Set watch: false in vitest config for CI/pre-commit contexts
   - Configure reporter for concise output in hook environment
   - Disable interactive features for automated execution

5. Configure test coverage thresholds
   - Set minimum coverage percentages (lines, functions, branches, statements)
   - Configure coverage reporting format for pre-commit output
   - Add coverage exclusion patterns for generated files

6. Add vitest patterns to lint-staged config
   - Define file patterns that should trigger vitest execution
   - Configure vitest command with appropriate flags
   - Set up conditional execution based on file types

7. Optimize test execution with parallelization settings
   - Configure maxConcurrency and pool settings
   - Set up CPU core detection for optimal thread allocation
   - Add memory usage optimization for large test suites
</info added on 2025-06-26T01:30:52.606Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Create sample tests and verify that only related tests run when specific files are staged. Measure execution time with various numbers of changed files.

subtasks.title: Integrate ts-prune and typedoc into pre-commit workflow
subtasks.description: Configure ts-prune for dead code detection and typedoc for documentation generation
subtasks.details:
Install ts-prune and typedoc as dev dependencies. Configure ts-prune to scan the entire codebase for unused exports. Set up typedoc.json with input/output paths and theme configuration. Create scripts to run ts-prune and generate warnings for unused code. Configure typedoc to generate documentation only for changed modules to optimize performance. Add both tools to lint-staged configuration with appropriate file patterns.
<info added on 2025-06-26T01:31:24.620Z>
Breaking down implementation into specific nano-tasks:

1. Install ts-prune and typedoc packages - Add both packages to devDependencies with npm install --save-dev ts-prune typedoc

2. Create ts-prune configuration file - Generate .tsprunerc or tsconfig-prune.json with project-specific ignore patterns and scanning rules

3. Write ts-prune detection script for unused exports - Create script that runs ts-prune and formats output for pre-commit consumption with appropriate exit codes

4. Create typedoc.json configuration - Configure input directories, output path, theme selection, and module resolution settings

5. Implement incremental doc generation for changed files - Build logic to detect changed TypeScript files and generate docs only for affected modules to improve performance

6. Add ts-prune and typedoc to lint-staged - Configure lint-staged patterns to run ts-prune on .ts/.tsx files and typedoc on documentation-relevant changes

7. Configure warning levels and error thresholds - Set up ts-prune to warn on unused exports but not fail commits, establish thresholds for when to block commits

8. Create documentation template customization - Customize typedoc theme and templates to match project branding and documentation standards
</info added on 2025-06-26T01:31:24.620Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Intentionally create unused exports and verify ts-prune detects them. Modify TypeScript files and confirm typedoc generates updated documentation.

subtasks.title: Create orchestrated pre-commit hook and documentation
subtasks.description: Build the final pre-commit hook that coordinates all tools and document the workflow
subtasks.details:
Create .husky/pre-commit script that runs lint-staged. Configure performance optimizations like running tools in parallel where possible. Implement execution time monitoring and logging. Add clear error messages and guidance for fixing common issues. Create bypass documentation explaining --no-verify flag usage. Write comprehensive CONTRIBUTING.md section explaining the pre-commit workflow. Create npm scripts for manual execution: lint, format, test:affected, check:unused, docs:generate. Add troubleshooting guide for common pre-commit failures.
<info added on 2025-06-26T01:31:53.434Z>
Nano-task breakdown:

1. Create main .husky/pre-commit script - Set up executable shell script that orchestrates all pre-commit checks with proper exit codes and error handling

2. Implement parallel execution strategy for tools - Use background processes and job control to run independent tools simultaneously, reducing total execution time

3. Add execution time monitoring and logging - Track and display timing for each tool, with colored output for performance insights and bottleneck identification

4. Create error message formatting and guidance - Design clear, actionable error messages with specific fix suggestions and relevant file context

5. Write CONTRIBUTING.md pre-commit section - Document the complete pre-commit workflow, expectations, and developer experience for new contributors

6. Document --no-verify bypass mechanism - Explain when and how to safely bypass pre-commit hooks, with warnings about responsibility and best practices

7. Create npm scripts for manual tool execution - Provide package.json scripts mirroring pre-commit tools for standalone developer use and CI integration

8. Build troubleshooting guide with common issues - Catalog frequent pre-commit failures with step-by-step resolution procedures and prevention tips

9. Implement performance optimization for large commits - Add file count thresholds, staged-only processing, and incremental checks to handle large changesets efficiently
</info added on 2025-06-26T01:31:53.434Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Run full pre-commit hook with various scenarios: clean commits, linting errors, test failures, unused exports. Measure total execution time and ensure it stays under 10 seconds for typical commits.
